description: Hard memory for exact terms, units, and key numbers by domain.

terms.definition: Core contributions typically fall into Insight, Performance, or Capability.
Establish territory, find a niche, and occupy the niche.
Total reader time spent resolving confusion; reduce by explaining concepts when introduced.
Efficiency of conveying effective information; avoid irrelevant history and redundancy.

key_values.value: Abstract → Introduction → Main body (Related Work, Methodology, Experiments, Discussion, Conclusion) → References.
At first occurrence, write the full term followed by the abbreviation in parentheses, unless the abbreviation is universally standard for the venue.
For each countable noun phrase, choose a/an, the, or zero article according to first mention, uniqueness, and genericity.
Open a sentence from the concept handed off by the previous sentence, and use the sentence ending to introduce the next concept.
Consistency of symbols/refs, figures cited in order, clear captions, anonymity and page limit checked.
When multiple technical nouns appear nearby, replace ambiguous pronouns with the explicit referent.
Use high-level taxonomy or problem-setting titles before method-level detail in survey subsections.
When sibling subsections compare representative methods, title them on one shared axis such as prerequisite information, modeled objects, or estimation target.
After a motivating example or numerical result, open the next paragraph by stating what the example has established and why a distinction, definition, or comparison now follows.

terms.definition: A location-indexed representation of channel-related knowledge that supports environment-aware wireless communication and sensing.

key_values.value: IEEE Wireless Communications Magazine.
Use prepositions that state the actual technical relation, such as based on, to assist, conditioned on, or inferred from, rather than vague phrases like relative to.
If a phrase such as 'stochastic refinement' does not make the mechanism legible, prefer a plainer term such as 'model refinement' and explain how measurements calibrate the model.
In side-by-side comparisons and tables, use paired labels on the same axis, such as grid-based and grid-less.
In survey sections, classify methods by information availability, inference mode, or problem setting before naming specific techniques.
Once a subsection title establishes the comparison axis, the opening sentences should restate the same axis instead of switching to a different classification standard.
If the section opener and the first subsection share the same motivation, merge the framing to avoid duplicated setup.
When discussing CSI burden versus channel dimensionality or dynamics, prefer direct wording such as 'scale poorly with' instead of 'keep pace with'.
When summarizing several methods, keep parallel syntax and state the scenario where each method is suitable so that the middle item bridges naturally between the first and third.
For simulation figures, place system configuration and parameter settings in the caption, and keep the main text focused on the compared schemes, dominant trend, and engineering implication.

If representative methods do not carry equal conceptual scope, do not force equal-weight sibling subsections; summarize narrower prior work together and give the broader method the main explanatory space.
In IEEE WCM prose, avoid opening a sentence with an unprepared concept; bridge from the previous sentence and split the prose if several new ideas need to be introduced.

terms.definition: A location-tagged representation of channel-related knowledge used to support environment-aware wireless communications and sensing.
Wireless communication design that exploits site-specific or environment-specific knowledge rather than relying only on instantaneous channel acquisition.
A CKM indexed from a fixed-position base station to arbitrary receivers or users.
A CKM indexed by both transmitter and receiver positions to characterize arbitrary Tx-Rx channel relations.
The reader time spent resolving missing links in logic; reduce it through local explanation and explicit paragraph handoffs.

units.rule: Use for gain, loss, and SNR-related reporting; keep baseline and delta definitions explicit.
Use for carrier frequency and band descriptions.
Use for distances, localization error, and deployment scale.
Use for latency and update-rate constraints.

key_values.value: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters.
The main body from Introduction to Conclusion must not exceed 4500 words.
The total number of figures and tables must not exceed 6.
Keep references within 15 whenever possible; exceeding 15 should be treated as exceptional.
Avoid formulas by default; if indispensable, use no more than 3 simple equations and explain each intuitively.
Wireless communication, wireless channel modeling, channel knowledge map, channel estimation, and physical-layer signal processing.
The introduction must state the practical problem, identify the technical gap, and summarize the contribution before entering method details.
The ending of one sentence or paragraph should set up the opening of the next whenever a concept, method, or phenomenon is being developed.
Chinese source material must be semantically rewritten into natural academic English rather than translated literally.

terms.definition: Establish territory, find a niche, and occupy the niche.
Total reader time spent resolving confusion; reduce by explaining concepts when introduced.
Efficiency of conveying effective information; avoid irrelevant history and redundancy.

key_values.value: Introduction → Background/Related Work → Methodology → Experiments/Results → Discussion → Conclusion.

terms.definition: Concise statement of goals and hypotheses that anchors the proposal.
Explicitly map proposal text to funder criteria and mission.
Concrete milestones, timeline, and risk mitigation to demonstrate achievability.
